# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our legacy Python 2.7 application. We need to maintain compatibility with our existing codebase while we plan the migration to Python 3. Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for container security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:2.7-slim-buster

RUN groupadd -r appgroup && useradd -r -g appgroup -d /home/appuser -m -s /usr/sbin/nologin appuser

RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        gcc \
        libpq-dev \
        libffi-dev \
        libssl-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

COPY requirements.txt .

RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --trusted-host pypi.python.org -r requirements.txt

RUN apt-get purge -y --auto-remove gcc && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

COPY . .

RUN chown -R appuser:appgroup /app && \
    chmod -R 550 /app

RUN find /app -name "*.pyc" -delete && \
    find /app -name "__pycache__" -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null; true

ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1

EXPOSE 8080

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \
    CMD python -c "import urllib2; urllib2.urlopen('http://localhost:8080/health')" || exit 1

USER appuser

CMD ["python", "app.py"]
